mechanism_narrative: A declining trend in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on average.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1 percent indicating near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month FEDFUNDS slope and forward 20-day SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1 percent reflecting elevated slack
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in more than 55% of triggered instances across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that jointly forecasts sustained economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; neither series alone captures the compounding demand effect.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change > 0 and RSAFS 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 604.0 thousand units and RSAFS at 389502 million dollars as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-RSAFS acceleration signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample rank IC by more than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue momentum that propagates quickly into short-horizon equity pricing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.0419 index value indicating below-100 output base
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly INDPRO change and subsequent 5-day SPX forward return is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3% while the federal funds rate remains below the level implied by a standard policy rule, markets anticipate future rate hikes that compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation > 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.268 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1 percent
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following triggered instances are not significantly lower than unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signals a tightening labor market that raises wage-cost concerns and forward inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both conditions co-occur, distinct from either series in isolation.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and UNRATE 3-month change < 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131132 thousand persons and UNRATE at 9.1 percent
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal shows no incremental predictive power over the single-series UNRATE signal (rank IC improvement < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion phase, positive quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) confirms durable aggregate demand strength that supports sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is expected to weaken or reverse outside expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 0 and RSAFS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.5 billion chained dollars as of latest release
regime_hint: expansion phase of the business cycle
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following triggered instances are not significantly higher than baseline during at least two separate expansion regimes (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign during contraction periods without being flagged as regime-dependent.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% over a trailing 3-month period signals an impending construction-sector slowdown that spills over into broader industrial supply chains, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, with the effect concentrated during housing cycle downturns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 604.0 thousand units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered instances show no significant negative deviation from baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct housing downturn episodes.
